ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-04-2021 Continued To Feb-03-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Aug-13-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion for an Order to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds & Court Reported Fee.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Reporter: Giselle Casey,CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Geoff Sloniker, Esq. appeared at today's hearing via CourtCall with plaintiffs Evelyn McKinzie, Fred McKinzie, III, Jeffrey McKinzie, Caleb McKinzie, Roy McKinzie, Jr., Kelik McKinzie, Eric McKinzie, Lamar McKinzie and Tenisha McKinzie, Wrongful Death Heirs of Fred McKinzie, Jr., deceased who were placed under oath and testified.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds from settlements from the underlying survival/wrongful death claims, in the following proportions:

Evelyn McKinzie - 20%
Fred McKinzie, III - 20%
Jeffrey McKinzie - 20%
Richard Bell - 20%
Caleb McKinzie, Roy McKinzie, Jr., Kelik McKinzie, Eric McKinzie, Lamar McKinzie and Tenisha McKinzie - 20%

All future net settlement funds from bankrupt defendants that have not yet settled, and those that have not yet paid agreed settlement sums, shall be distrubuted to the decendents' heirs in the percentages set forth above.

The signature of plaintiff Evelyn McKinzie alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest For Deceased Plaintiff
Off Calendar May-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party Fred McKinzie.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER SUBSTITUTING SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST FOR DECEASED PLAINTIFF

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest for Deceased Plaintiff is granted. Fred McKinzie, III is substituted as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff, Fred McKinzie, Jr. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party Fred McKinzie Jr.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
No opposition and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is Monday, March 4, 2019 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is March 13, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 28, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 15, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 1, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 14, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Notice Of Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than November 27, 2018 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than November 27, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on January 16, 2019 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than January 18, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 1, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by February 22, 2019 at 12:00p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, February 28, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
